reviews.review_description: I'm 66 years old. I'm tired of boss fights. I'm tired of getting killed 40 or 50 times in a game. I've recently discovered puzzle games, and this was a good one. Sometimes it gets a little frustrating but it's super satisfying When you figure them out, good job, guys.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your review! Very happy you liked the puzzles and we hope you keep on playing it! Reviews like this one help us create more levels and more gameplay time.

reviews.review_title: In space, no one can hear you puzzle
reviews.review_description:
Avo : Echoes of the Void is a good Introduction to the puzzle genre in VR, it's not too difficult and you won't get stuck for too long before being able to progress.
There are four areas to complete, depending on your level of experience with puzzle games you could whip through in a few hours.
The movement is mostly a swimming type or motion as you move around in zero gravity, but around half way through you unlock a little booster for your hand, which then is move akin to a driving type of movement, you can combine the two for more intricate moves when you need to get closer to something to see it.
I don't want to say much else, I wouldn't want to spoil it, but I would say it has a good variety of puzzles with no replications that I can recall, gentle pace, not too difficult and a well rounded budget title.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind feedback! Happy you enjoyed it

reviews.review_description:
You first have to get familiar with the zero gravity movement. Once you get used to it its quite comfortable.

In each part of the game you get new ways to move around. This is unique and a lot of fun.

The puzzles are challenging but not too hard. I think an average player will need around 4 hours to get through the game.

It was fun to dive into the game and get caught by the atmosphere. Hopefully we will see part 3.

developer_response.body: Yes part 3 will be definitely on the map! I hope more people will request it like you did :)

reviews.review_description:
AVO: Echoes of the Void is a fun escape-room experience set in space, featuring unique movement mechanics that let you float and navigate in zero gravity.

The sensation of being in space is very well executed, and the atmosphere helps create an immersive sci-fi environment while you solve puzzles and explore each room. Movement feels natural and adds a refreshing twist compared to traditional escape-room games.

I managed to complete a few rooms, but the difficulty clearly ramps up as you progress, with fewer clues and more challenging puzzles.

Overall, it’s a solid VR escape-room title with great immersion and clever mechanics. A great pick for puzzle fans who enjoy a good challenge in space. 🌌🔐

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind review. Yes the difficulty increases with time, but I am sure you'll crack all of the puzzles!

reviews.review_description:
If you're looking for a puzzle adventure game then Avo: Echoes of the Void has you covered. A VR puzzle game set on a mysterious, abandoned spacecraft. The atmosphere is thick, the puzzles are mind-bending, and the "Void" is always watching.

First Impressions: Exploring the hauntingly beautiful ship.
Gameplay: Solving complex environmental puzzles in VR, with great VR interactions.
The Mystery: Piecing together what happened to the crew.
If you’re a fan of sci-fi mysteries or atmospheric VR titles, this is a must-buy!

Head over to my YouTube channel for my first impressions gameplay!
😁✌️☮️❤️

developer_response.body: Love the title! Well done :) and thanks for playing, we loved that you enjoyed it

reviews.review_description: I've played the first of what I believe is four rooms. I'm enjoying myself! The puzzles took me longer than I'd like to admit. If you like puzzle games or escape rooms, check this one out. Don't be worried about motion sickness in a zero gravity game. The movement is quite subtle, so you're not being whipped around.

developer_response.body: Super happy you enjoyed the game! Thank you for playing!

reviews.review_description:
I recently had the chance to dive into AVO – Echoes of the Void, and I can confidently say it’s a standout experience that builds beautifully on the foundation laid by the studio’s first game, AVO – Escape Space. As someone who was fortunate enough to be involved in the creation and testing of the original title, seeing how far the developers have come is both impressive and genuinely rewarding.

AVO – Escape Space had a certain raw charm to it. During development, there was a clear sense of ambition and creativity driving the project forward. Being part of the testing process gave me a unique perspective on how much care went into refining mechanics, balancing gameplay, and shaping the overall player experience. It wasn’t just about fixing bugs it was about helping bring a vision to life. Watching that early concept evolve into a fully realized game was an absolute pleasure.

Fast forward to Echoes of the Void, and you can really see that growth. The game feels more polished, more confident, and more immersive in every aspect. I absolutely love the new more realistic movement! The atmosphere is richer, the mechanics are tighter, and the storytelling carries a deeper emotional weight. It’s clear the team learned from their first project and pushed themselves to elevate every element.

What stands out most is how the spirit of the original game is still present. There’s that same sense of exploration and curiosity, but now it’s supported by more refined design and a stronger narrative direction. It feels like a natural evolution rather than a complete departure, which makes it especially satisfying for those who experienced the journey from the beginning.

Overall, AVO – Echoes of the Void is not just a great game it’s a testament to growth, passion, and dedication. Having been part of the early stages with Escape Space, it’s incredibly fulfilling to see how far the project has come. I’m excited to see what this team does next.

developer_response.body: What a great review! We're humbled!

reviews.review_title: Escape Room Puzzle Game In Space
reviews.review_description: If you're looking for a Escape Room Type puzzle adventure game that still gives you 5 to 6 hours of gameplay then Avo: Echoes of the Void has you covered. The game takes place in a Spaceship, where all systems are broken and you wake up trying to find out what happened. The game is like an escape room where each room has puzzles and things to figure out before you progress forward. What's different is the story being told as you do this. Making it way more fun to play, and it not being a short experience like most escape room type games. Worthy to check out if your into the Genre and unique in the space.

I’ve been playing this game for a few days now.

I’m a big fan of escape games and puzzles, and this one really caught my attention. I really like the graphics and the gravity mechanics, they feel smooth and immersive.

I haven’t progressed very far yet, but the game already feels exciting and engaging.

Definitely recommend it! 👍

The mechanics of movement at first are a little different but when you get used to it they are fine.

Each room has tasks to do with a little help and obviously getting harder as you go along.

I find the game great fun to play and keeps my mind active whilst having fun ( I am a little older than your average vr gamer….lol)

I look forward to what my come next and continuing to play the current version

Swimlifeuk

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind review, very happy you loved the mechanics

reviews.review_description:
You wake up on a wrecked spaceship. You float through the spaceship trying to solve puzzles. It's similar to games like Red Matter. But instead of walking you float through the ship. The movement takes a bit to get used to. Kind of a rowing/swimming motion. I play lots puzzle type games. This is a fun one. I haven't completed it yet. Some of the puzzles have definitely been challenging for me. The movements make it a unique experience. The graphics are good. 
It's listed as comfortable but I could see how some might have issues with floating. I do get motion sickness sometimes with other games but did not get it while playing this game.

developer_response.body: We really focused to make it as comfortable as possible! Happy you liked it

reviews.review_description:
AVO: Echoes of the Void is a really immersive and memorable VR experience. The atmosphere is beautifully crafted, with a strong sense of mystery that keeps you engaged from start to finish. The environments feel polished and alive, and the sound design adds a lot to the overall depth of the game.

What stands out the most is how interactive and intuitive everything feels. The puzzles are clever without being frustrating, and the mechanics are satisfying to use.

Overall, it’s a very solid and enjoyable VR title that shows real creativity and ambition. Definitely worth trying if you enjoy immersive, story-driven experiences.

AVO: Echoes of the Void offers a mysterious and immersive VR exploration experience. Its focus on discovery and interaction creates a unique atmosphere that encourages curiosity. A promising concept with strong potential for players who enjoy exploratory VR experiences.
A fairly difficult escape game in the “Lost in space” atmosphere where you are left to your own devices...

developer_response.body: We love being lost in space!

reviews.review_description:
This game completely surprised me. From the moment I started playing. The levels are very nicely detailed. You can tell the developers put a lot of care into the environments. Every area feels alive and thoughtfully designed instead of just being empty space. It really adds to the immersion and makes you want to explore everything around you.

Moving in this game is not your typical VR movement you see everywhere. Once you get used to it, it’s honestly amazing, and actually really fun to use. it makes the game stand out from a lot of other VR games.

One thing that stood out for me the most was the objects. Picking things up, holding them, and using them feels incredibly satisfying. You can throw it around and it will like float. The physics in this game are so nice, you’ll find yourself just messing around with objects because it’s that enjoyable.

The game is definitely challenging, but in a good way. It’s not frustratingly hard, it’s the kind of difficulty that makes you think and take your time. Nothing is just handed to you, and you actually have to figure things out yourself. That’s what makes it so satisfying when you finally get it right, which makes the whole experience way more enjoyable.

Overall, this game is an easy 5 stars it’s incredibly immersive, polished, and just genuinely fun to play. 🔥🔥🔥

4 rooms to discover. When the puzzles are solved in 1 room, you can go to the next room. 
For locomotion, you are really ‘ floating’. And you have to use your hands to move. So this is also unique.

I like the concept. Restore the spaceship, solve puzzles.

I enjoyed every minute in game.

developer_response.body: Very happy you liked it! We enjoyed every minute of making it as well :)

reviews.review_description: Awesome game takes a few minutes to get used to the movement but that's just like the first puzzle of the game it's very rewarding getting through everything because it seems very difficult but then easy once figured out :D if your a heavy gamer you could finish it in a day but I'm not the brightest so it's taking me a little longer haha worth it tho 100% thanks for great game and great concept!!!

developer_response.body: It took me 3 days to finish.. :)

reviews.review_description: This game is a puzzle game set in space where you go from one room to the next by solving puzzles to advance. The graphics are pretty good and the puzzles aren’t too complicated in my opinion. The coolest part was in the beginning when you’re floating in outer space and trying to get to a vortex to be transported. I will have to say the movement is quite the learning curve and may require precise movements. The movement is basically swimming but you hold down and quick release both triggers. In a snapping motion and don’t hold on (that was my mistake). Alot of times, when pushing forward chest level, I’d find myself rising diagonally instead of moving forward. However, I learned to start back more behind my back tha mid chest if that makes  sense. Feels like a lot of strokes for a little bit of short burst movement. Besides these small learning curves, it’s a visually great puzzle game. Would recommend!

developer_response.body: Hello! thanks for the review. We added a clearer tutorial for the movements in the beginning. thanks for the hint!

reviews.review_description:
As a fan of high-caliber VR titles like Red Matter, I found this game to be a fantastic experience. The atmosphere is deeply immersive, and the progression feels natural and well-paced from start to finish.

What makes this title truly unique is how your movement evolves. Each section introduces new ways to navigate the environment, which keeps the gameplay feeling fresh and inventive. While the control scheme in the initial part of the game can take a little bit of time to master, it is absolutely worth the effort; once it clicks, it becomes a highly rewarding and fun part of the experience.

The puzzles are excellent and require genuine critical thinking. You are encouraged to experiment with your tools and pay close attention to your surroundings, making the 'aha!' moments incredibly satisfying. 

It was a joy to play and I am already hoping for a Part 3 and enjoyed this so much that I am going back to purchase Part 1!

developer_response.body: Happy to be compared to big games :) Yes part 3 will should be in the plans :)

reviews.review_description: Looks like a fun game with lots of fun puzzles. I couldn't play it long enough to see them though. The movement mechanics are unintuitive and tiring. My shoulders, elbows, and wrists hurt. I guess I'm too unfit for this game. Add to that a lack hints system and you get frustration. I loved the level design and concept. I wish you guys would consider a more accessible movement system as an alternative.

developer_response.body: Hi Majed6, thanks for your note. Happy to say that we're adding new options for the controllers, it should be done within this month. We're happy as well that you liked the game design and the puzzles, and would love to hear your feedback after you play with more controller options soon!

reviews.review_description: As a Star Wars fan this felt like I was on a solo mission, solving puzzles and a ship all by myself. A little creepy since you are alone, but the movement is awesome, and it is action packed! Recommend.
